tool.name: bruteforce_subdomains
tool.description:
Perform async subdomain brute-forcing with DNS resolution.
This is an ACTIVE reconnaissance tool - it makes DNS queries against public DNS resolvers to discover subdomains.
Use this after passive enumeration (crt.sh) to find additional subdomains not listed in certificate transparency logs.

parameters.parameter: Target domain to bruteforce (e.g., "example.com")
Path to custom wordlist file (one subdomain per line). If not provided, uses built-in wordlist.
Built-in wordlist size: "small" (500 entries), "medium" (5000 entries), "large" (30000 entries). Default: "medium"
Maximum concurrent DNS queries. Default: 50. Reduced automatically in stealth mode.
Enable stealth mode with reduced speed and randomized timing. Default: false
List of already discovered subdomains (from crt.sh) for deduplication.
Custom list of subdomain prefixes to test instead of built-in wordlist.

tool.returns: - success: Whether the scan completed - domain: The target domain - discovered: List of discovered subdomains with IP addresses - total_checked: Number of subdomains checked - wildcard_detected: Whether wildcard DNS was detected - new_discoveries: Subdomains not in existing_subdomains
tool.example:
# Basic subdomain bruteforce result = bruteforce_subdomains(domain="example.com")
# With deduplication against crt.sh findings result = bruteforce_subdomains( domain="example.com", existing_subdomains=["www.example.com", "mail.example.com"], wordlist_size="medium" )
# Stealth mode for sensitive targets result = bruteforce_subdomains(domain="target.com", stealth=True)

tool.name: smart_subdomain_gen
tool.description:
Generate intelligent subdomain prefixes based on target context.
Uses technology stack, industry, and pattern analysis to generate likely subdomain prefixes. More targeted than generic wordlists.

parameters.parameter: Target domain (e.g., "acme-corp.com")
Detected technologies (e.g., ["nginx", "php", "mysql", "wordpress"])
Industry vertical (e.g., "finance", "healthcare", "ecommerce")
Already discovered subdomains for pattern learning
Maximum prefixes to generate. Default: 100

tool.returns: - suggestions: List of suggested subdomain prefixes - reasoning: Why each category was suggested - priority: Priority ordering hints
tool.example: # Generate based on tech stack result = smart_subdomain_gen( domain="example.com", tech_stack=["wordpress", "mysql", "nginx"], industry="ecommerce" )

tool.name: run_subdomain_tools
tool.description:
Run external subdomain enumeration tools (subfinder, assetfinder).
Executes available external tools and aggregates their results. Requires the tools to be installed on the system PATH.

parameters.parameter: Target domain (e.g., "example.com")
List of tools to run. Default: ["subfinder", "assetfinder"]
Timeout for each tool. Default: 120

tool.returns: - subdomains: Combined list of discovered subdomains - by_tool: Results broken down by tool

tool.name: comprehensive_subdomain_enum
tool.description:
Run comprehensive subdomain enumeration combining multiple techniques.
Orchestrates passive enumeration, external tools, smart wordlist generation, and active DNS brute-forcing into a single comprehensive scan.

parameters.parameter: Target domain (e.g., "example.com")
Enable stealth mode (slower, more careful). Default: false
Include DNS brute-forcing phase. Default: true
Wordlist size for bruteforce: "small", "medium", "large". Default: "medium"
Try to use subfinder/assetfinder if available. Default: true

tool.returns: - subdomains: All discovered subdomains - by_source: Results broken down by technique - sources_used: Which techniques were used
